source: Rocky Linux Admin Guide, ch. 7.5 Guest management (pp. 114-115)

questions.q: Add the user patrick to the group GroupA (as a supplementary group) using gpasswd.
questions.why: gpasswd -a adds a user to a group as a supplementary member.

questions.q: Make alain an administrator of the group GroupA using gpasswd.
questions.why: gpasswd -A sets the list of administrative users for the group.

questions.q: Remove the user patrick from the group GroupA using gpasswd.
questions.why: gpasswd -d removes a user from the group.

options: -M SETS the entire member list (replacing it); -a ADDS a single user
-M adds one user; -a replaces the whole list
questions.why: gpasswd -M defines the complete membership list (a modification/overwrite), whereas -a appends one user.

questions.q: Display the UID, primary group and supplementary groups of the user alain.
questions.why: id shows a user's UID, primary GID and the groups they belong to.

questions.q: Temporarily switch your primary group to the supplementary group groupA (spawning a new child shell).
questions.why: newgrp selects one of your supplementary groups as a temporary primary group; each switch starts a new child shell (exit returns you).

questions.q: After 'newgrp groupA', files you create take which group, and what happens to the shell?
options: New files get groupA as their group, and you are in a new child shell (SHLVL increases)
New files keep your old primary group, and no new shell is started
questions.why: newgrp makes groupA the temporary primary group so new files belong to it, and it spawns a child shell (unlike su -, which does not).
